global_config.visual_style: Cinematic, realistic, bright and sunny day, slice-of-life aesthetic, vibrant colors.
global_config.setting_summary_cn: 四个国际学生朋友在春天的北京，决定骑自行车去郊区爬山，享受周末和讨论环保。
global_config.setting_summary_en: Four international student friends in Beijing on a spring day decide to go mountain climbing in the suburbs by bike, enjoying their weekend and discussing environmental protection.

character_definitions.name: 张天明
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A friendly and energetic Chinese-American man in his early 20s, with short black hair, wearing casual sportswear like a t-shirt and shorts.

character_definitions.name: 丽莎
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A curious and cheerful American woman in her early 20s, with blonde hair tied in a ponytail, wearing bright-colored athletic clothing.

character_definitions.name: 雪梅
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A thoughtful and calm Chinese woman in her early 20s, with long dark hair, wearing comfortable and practical outdoor attire.

character_definitions.name: 柯林
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A practical and smart American man in his early 20s, with light brown hair, wearing glasses and a polo shirt.

character_definitions.name: 马克
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A tall, athletic German man in his early 20s, with short blond hair, looking adventurous and ready for a hike.

hanzi: 张天明他们四个人来北京已经两三个月了,天气也逐渐暖和了。他们每天上课的上课,找工作的找工作,半个月没见面了。今天是星期六,大家都想轻松轻松,天明建议去爬山,丽莎和雪梅不反对,柯林也觉得这个建议不错,他说正好他的德国朋友马克也想去。
pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng tāmen sì ge rén lái Běijīng yǐjīng liǎng sān ge yuè le tiānqì yě zhújiàn nuǎnhuo le tāmen měitiān shàng kè de shàng kè zhǎo gōngzuò de zhǎo gōngzuò bàn ge yuè méi jiàn miàn le jīntiān shì xīngqīliù dàjiā dōu xiǎng qīngsōng qīngsōng Tiānmíng jiànyì qù pá shān Lìshā hé Xuěméi bù fǎnduì Kē Lín yě juéde zhège jiànyì búcuò tā shuō zhènghǎo tā de Déguó péngyou Mǎkè yě xiǎng qù
translation: It has been two or three months since Zhang Tianming and his three friends came to Beijing, and the weather has gradually gotten warmer. Every day, they were busy with classes or job hunting and hadn't seen each other for half a month. Today is Saturday, and everyone wants to relax. Tianming suggested going mountain climbing. Lisha and Xuemei didn't object, and Ke Lin also thought it was a good idea, saying that his German friend Mark also wanted to go.

scenes.scene_goal: 介绍人物背景，引出周末爬山的计划。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 快速蒙太奇展示张天明、丽莎、雪梅、柯林在北京的忙碌生活（上课、在图书馆学习、浏览招聘网站）。然后镜头转到周六，四人聚在一个阳光明媚的学生休息室里。张天明兴奋地提议去爬山，大家立刻表示赞同。
scenes.setting_en: A quick montage of busy student life in Beijing, then a bright, modern student lounge on a sunny Saturday.
scenes.camera_movement: Starts with quick cuts for the montage, then a stable medium shot of the group, followed by close-ups on their happy faces.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Quick montage: a young Asian man in a university classroom, a white woman scrolling through job websites on a laptop, an Asian woman studying in a library. Transition to a sunny Saturday: the four friends are gathered in a cozy student lounge, laughing. The weather outside is warm. Zhang Tianming enthusiastically suggests an idea. Lisha, Xuemei, and Ke Lin all nod and smile in agreement. Slice-of-life, cinematic, natural lighting.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 张天明他们四个人来北京已经两三个月了,天气也逐渐暖和了。他们每天上课的上课,找工作的找工作,半个月没见面了。今天是星期六,大家都想轻松轻松,天明建议去爬山,丽莎和雪梅不反对,柯林也觉得这个建议不错,他说正好他的德国朋友马克也想去。
voice_over.pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng tāmen sì ge rén lái Běijīng yǐjīng liǎng sān ge yuè le tiānqì yě zhújiàn nuǎnhuo le tāmen měitiān shàng kè de shàng kè zhǎo gōngzuò de zhǎo gōngzuò bàn ge yuè méi jiàn miàn le jīntiān shì xīngqīliù dàjiā dōu xiǎng qīngsōng qīngsōng Tiānmíng jiànyì qù pá shān Lìshā hé Xuěméi bù fǎnduì Kē Lín yě juéde zhège jiànyì búcuò tā shuō zhènghǎo tā de Déguó péngyou Mǎkè yě xiǎng qù
voice_over.translation: It has been two or three months since Zhang Tianming and his three friends came to Beijing, and the weather has gradually gotten warmer. Every day, they were busy with classes or job hunting and hadn't seen each other for half a month. Today is Saturday, and everyone wants to relax. Tianming suggested going mountain climbing. Lisha and Xuemei didn't object, and Ke Lin also thought it was a good idea, saying that his German friend Mark also wanted to go.

hanzi: 可是怎么去呢?坐出租车吧,太贵,而且也没意思;坐公交车吧,人太多,太挤。柯林想出了一个主意,骑自行车!大家马上就同意了,觉得骑自行车又能锻炼身体,又省钱,而且有益于环境保护。
pinyin: kěshì zěnme qù ne zuò chūzūchē ba tài guì érqiě yě méi yìsi zuò gōngjiāochē ba rén tài duō tài jǐ Kē Lín xiǎng chū le yí ge zhúyi qí zìxíngchē dàjiā mǎshàng jiù tóngyì le juéde qí zìxíngchē yòu néng duànliàn shēntǐ yòu shěng qián érqiě yǒuyìyú huánjìng bǎohù
translation: But how should they get there? Taking a taxi would be too expensive and boring; taking the bus would be too crowded with too many people. Ke Lin came up with an idea: ride bikes! Everyone immediately agreed, feeling that riding bikes is not only good exercise and saves money, but is also beneficial for environmental protection.

scenes.scene_goal: 展现朋友们讨论并决定交通方式的过程，突出骑自行车的优点。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 朋友们继续讨论，脸上露出思索的表情。画面中出现有趣的动画气泡：一个显示飞走的钞票和出租车，另一个显示被挤成沙丁鱼的公交车。柯林眼前一亮，做出骑车的动作。大家立刻兴奋起来，互相击掌庆祝这个好主意。
scenes.setting_en: The same student lounge as Scene 1.
scenes.camera_movement: Quick zoom-ins on characters' thoughtful faces, then a whip pan to Ke Lin as he gets the idea.
scenes.video_prompt_en: In the student lounge, the friends ponder. A cartoon thought bubble appears over Lisha's head showing a taxi and money with wings flying away. Another bubble appears over Xuemei's head showing people crammed into a bus. Ke Lin's eyes light up, he snaps his fingers and mimes riding a bike. The others' faces brighten, and they all high-five, excited about the idea. Fun, energetic, with stylized animated elements.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 可是怎么去呢?坐出租车吧,太贵,而且也没意思;坐公交车吧,人太多,太挤。柯林想出了一个主意,骑自行车!大家马上就同意了,觉得骑自行车又能锻炼身体,又省钱,而且有益于环境保护。
voice_over.pinyin: kěshì zěnme qù ne zuò chūzūchē ba tài guì érqiě yě méi yìsi zuò gōngjiāochē ba rén tài duō tài jǐ Kē Lín xiǎng chū le yí ge zhúyi qí zìxíngchē dàjiā mǎshàng jiù tóngyì le juéde qí zìxíngchē yòu néng duànliàn shēntǐ yòu shěng qián érqiě yǒuyìyú huánjìng bǎohù
voice_over.translation: But how should they get there? Taking a taxi would be too expensive and boring; taking the bus would be too crowded with too many people. Ke Lin came up with an idea: ride bikes! Everyone immediately agreed, feeling that riding bikes is not only good exercise and saves money, but is also beneficial for environmental protection.

hanzi: 他们早上很早就出发了。骑了很长的一段路以后,有点热,也有点累,就下了车,一边推着车,一边聊了起来。
pinyin: tāmen zǎoshang hěn zǎo jiù chūfā le qí le hěn cháng de yí duàn lù yǐhòu yǒudiǎn rè yě yǒudiǎn lèi jiù xià le chē yìbiān tuī zhe chē yìbiān liáo le qǐlái
translation: They set out very early in the morning. After riding for a long way, they got a bit hot and tired, so they got off their bikes, pushing them while they chatted.

scenes.scene_goal: 展示朋友们开始骑行之旅，并从骑行过渡到推车散步的场景。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 场景切换到北京郊外一条风景优美的公路上。清晨的阳光下，五个朋友（包括新加入的马克）精神饱满地骑着自行车。一组快速剪辑的骑行镜头后，画面节奏放慢，他们额头微微出汗，看起来有些累了。他们下车，开始在路边推着自行车悠闲地散步和聊天。
scenes.setting_en: A scenic, paved road on the outskirts of Beijing, with green hills in the distance. Early morning, golden hour light.
scenes.camera_movement: Dynamic tracking shot following the cyclists, then a slower, steady shot from the side as they walk and push their bikes.
scenes.shot_type: Wide Shot, Tracking Shot, Medium Long Shot
scenes.video_prompt_en: Early morning, golden hour. A group of five diverse young adults (three men, two women) are joyfully riding bicycles on a scenic road leading out of Beijing. Drone shot from above showing them riding in a line. Cut to a shot of them looking a bit tired but happy, wiping sweat from their foreheads. They dismount their bikes and begin to walk slowly, pushing the bikes alongside them and chatting casually. The atmosphere is relaxed and pleasant. Beautiful natural landscape.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 他们早上很早就出发了。骑了很长的一段路以后,有点热,也有点累,就下了车,一边推着车,一边聊了起来。
voice_over.pinyin: tāmen zǎoshang hěn zǎo jiù chūfā le qí le hěn cháng de yí duàn lù yǐhòu yǒudiǎn rè yě yǒudiǎn lèi jiù xià le chē yìbiān tuī zhe chē yìbiān liáo le qǐlái
voice_over.translation: They set out very early in the morning. After riding for a long way, they got a bit hot and tired, so they got off their bikes, pushing them while they chatted.

hanzi: 还有汽车,不但要用很多能源,而且还对空气造成严重污染。
大家都应该像我们这样,少开车、多骑车、多走路,又环保节能、又有益于健康。
pinyin: háiyǒu qìchē bùdàn yào yòng hěnduō néngyuán érqiě hái duì kōngqì zàochéng yánzhòng wūrǎn
dàjiā dōu yīnggāi xiàng wǒmen zhèyàng shǎo kāichē duō qí chē duō zǒulù yòu huánbǎo jiénéng yòu yǒuyìyú jiànkāng
translation: And then there are cars. Not only do they consume a lot of energy, but they also cause serious air pollution.
Everyone should be like us: drive less, ride bikes more, walk more. It's both environmentally friendly, energy-saving, and good for your health.

scenes.scene_goal: 通过视觉对比，展现汽车污染的坏处和绿色出行的好处。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头从小明和小丽在公园小径上轻松行走开始。一辆汽车从他们身边驶过，扬起尾气。画面色调变灰暗，特写汽车排气管。紧接着，画面切换为明亮的蒙太奇：人们在阳光下骑自行车、在林荫道上散步，充满活力。最后镜头回到小明和小丽，他们相视一笑。
scenes.setting_en: A park path, then a brief shot of a car exhaust, followed by a montage of a sunny city park and walking trails.
scenes.camera_movement: Tracking shot, then a sudden zoom to exhaust pipe, followed by dynamic quick cuts, ending on a static medium shot.
scenes.shot_type: Medium shot, extreme close-up, wide shot
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic anime style. A young Asian man and woman walk on a park path. A car passes, shot becomes greyish, extreme close-up on the car's exhaust pipe emitting smoke. Quick cut to a bright, sunny montage: people cheerfully riding bikes, people walking briskly on a tree-lined path. Final shot: the man and woman look at each other and smile.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 还有汽车,不但要用很多能源,而且还对空气造成严重污染。大家都应该像我们这样,少开车、多骑车、多走路,又环保节能、又有益于健康。
voice_over.pinyin: háiyǒu qìchē bùdàn yào yòng hěnduō néngyuán érqiě hái duì kōngqì zàochéng yánzhòng wūrǎn 。 dàjiā dōu yīnggāi xiàng wǒmen zhèyàng shǎo kāichē duō qí chē duō zǒulù yòu huánbǎo jiénéng yòu yǒuyìyú jiànkāng 。
voice_over.translation: And then there are cars. Not only do they consume a lot of energy, but they also cause serious air pollution. Everyone should be like us: drive less, ride bikes more, walk more. It's both environmentally friendly, energy-saving, and good for your health.

hanzi: 对,地球是我们的家,我们应该好好保护它!
我们应该从小地方做起,比如随手关灯,节约用水。
pinyin: duì dì qiú shì wǒ men de jiā wǒ men yīng gāi hǎo hǎo bǎo hù tā
wǒ men yīng gāi cóng xiǎo dì fang zuò qǐ bǐ rú suí shǒu guān dēng jié yuē yòng shuǐ
translation: Right, the Earth is our home. We should take good care of it!
We should start with the small things, like turning off lights when we leave a room and conserving water.

scenes.scene_goal: 强调保护地球要从生活中的小事做起，并表达认同感。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头从小明和小丽的笑脸，缓缓拉远，展现他们身后美丽的地球的缩影（特效）。然后画面叠化，出现一只手关掉电灯开关，另一只手关紧水龙头的特写。最后，小明和小丽在画面中互相竖起大拇指，表示赞同。
scenes.setting_en: The park path, transitioning to abstract/symbolic imagery.
scenes.camera_movement: Slow pull-out shot revealing a stylized Earth behind the characters (special effect). Dissolve to close-ups. End on a static medium shot.
scenes.shot_type: Medium shot, wide shot (with VFX), close-up
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic anime style. Start on the smiling faces of the young Asian man and woman. Camera pulls back to reveal a beautiful, stylized image of planet Earth glowing softly behind them. Dissolve to a close-up of a hand turning off a light switch. Dissolve to another close-up of a hand turning off a dripping water faucet. Cut back to the man and woman giving each other a thumbs-up, looking determined and positive.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 对,地球是我们的家,我们应该好好保护它! 我们应该从小地方做起,比如随手关灯,节约用水。好!同意!
voice_over.pinyin: duì dì qiú shì wǒ men de jiā wǒ men yīng gāi hǎo hǎo bǎo hù tā ! wǒ men yīng gāi cóng xiǎo dì fang zuò qǐ bǐ rú suí shǒu guān dēng jié yuē yòng shuǐ 。 hǎo ! tóng yì !
voice_over.translation: Right, the Earth is our home. We should take good care of it! We should start with the small things, like turning off lights when we leave a room and conserving water. Okay! Agreed!

hanzi: 哎,你们看,都到了山下了。我们开始爬山吧,看谁先到山上!加油!
pinyin: āi nǐ men kàn dōu dào le shān xià le wǒ men kāi shǐ pá shān ba kàn shéi xiān dào shān shàng jiā yóu
translation: Hey, look, we've reached the foot of the mountain. Let's start climbing. Let's see who gets to the top first! Let's go!

scenes.scene_goal: 以一个充满活力的行动结束讨论，将环保和健康生活方式联系起来。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 小明和小丽已经走到了山脚下，小明兴奋地指着山顶，对小丽做出一个“我们比赛吧”的挑战手势。小丽笑着接受挑战，两人摆出起跑的姿势，充满活力地喊着“加油！”，然后一起向山上跑去。
scenes.setting_en: The foot of a lush, green mountain with a clear trail leading up.
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot establishing the location, then a quick zoom towards their excited faces, ending with a low-angle shot as they start running up the trail.
scenes.shot_type: Wide shot, medium close-up, low-angle shot
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic anime style, wide shot of a young Asian man and woman standing at the foot of a beautiful green mountain. The man points excitedly towards the summit and gestures a challenge to the woman. She laughs and nods, getting into a runner's starting pose. They both look energetic and shout 'Jia you!' then start running up the mountain trail. Low angle shot captures their dynamic movement.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 哎,你们看,都到了山下了。我们开始爬山吧,看谁先到山上!加油!
voice_over.pinyin: āi nǐ men kàn dōu dào le shān xià le wǒ men kāi shǐ pá shān ba kàn shéi xiān dào shān shàng jiā yóu !
voice_over.translation: Hey, look, we've reached the foot of the mountain. Let's start climbing. Let's see who gets to the top first! Let's go!

global_config.teaching_style: A friendly, encouraging, and clear instructional style that breaks down complex concepts into simple, digestible parts. It focuses on practical usage and cultural context.
global_config.visual_style: Clean, modern, and card-based, with clear typography and animated highlights to focus on key information.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand the background of the story: the characters, the time, and the weather.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's start with the first sentence. It sets the scene for our story. It tells us that Zhang Tianming and his friends have been in Beijing for two or three months, and the weather is getting warmer. The phrase [zh:已经...了] means 'already'. Here, [zh:已经两三个月了] means 'it has already been two or three months'. The word [zh:逐渐] means 'gradually', so [zh:逐渐暖和了] tells us the weather is gradually getting warmer.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 张天明他们四个人来北京已经两三个月了,天气也逐渐暖和了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng tāmen sì ge rén lái Běijīng yǐjīng liǎng sān ge yuè le, tiānqì yě zhújiàn nuǎnhuo le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: It has been two or three months since Zhang Tianming and his four friends came to Beijing, and the weather has gradually gotten warmer.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb indicating a slow, step-by-step change over time.
Used to describe pleasantly warm weather or temperature.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a calendar flipping through 2-3 months, and a sun icon appearing to show warming weather.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn a structure to describe different people being busy with different activities.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence describes what everyone has been up to. Notice the special structure: [zh:上课的上课, 找工作的找工作]. This pattern, 'Verb 1 的 Verb 1, Verb 2 的 Verb 2', is used to say that different people in a group are all busy doing their own things. So, 'some were busy with classes, and others were busy looking for jobs'. As a result, they haven't seen each other for half a month, or [zh:半个月].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他们每天上课的上课,找工作的找工作,半个月没见面了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tāmen měitiān shàng kè de shàng kè, zhǎo gōngzuò de zhǎo gōngzuò, bàn ge yuè méi jiàn miàn le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Every day, some were busy with classes and some were busy job hunting, and they hadn't seen each other for half a month.
on_screen_text.notes: Grammar point: Verb 1 的 Verb 1, Verb 2 的 Verb 2

grammar_points.pattern: Verb 1 的 verb 1, verb 2 的 verb 2
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Verb 1 de verb 1, verb 2 de verb 2
grammar_points.explanation_en: This structure implies that everyone is occupied doing his/her own thing; no one is idle.

segments.visual_notes: Split screen animation: one side shows a person in a classroom, the other shows a person at a job interview.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to suggest an activity and use adjective reduplication to express 'doing something to achieve a state'.

narration.subtitle_en: Now it's Saturday, and everyone wants to relax. The phrase [zh:轻松轻松] is a great example of adjective reduplication. It means 'to relax a bit' or 'to do something to become relaxed'. Tianming then makes a suggestion, using the verb [zh:建议], which means 'to suggest'. His idea is to go mountain climbing, or [zh:爬山].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 今天是星期六,大家都想轻松轻松,天明建议去爬山。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Jīntiān shì xīngqīliù, dàjiā dōu xiǎng qīngsōng qīngsōng, Tiānmíng jiànyì qù páshān.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Today is Saturday, and everyone wants to relax. Tianming suggested going mountain climbing.
on_screen_text.notes: Grammar point: Adjective Reduplication (XYXY)

highlight_words.explanation_en: As a verb (reduplicated), it means 'to relax'.
Can be used as both a verb and a noun.

highlight_words.translation: to climb a mountain, to hike
highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object compound: 爬 (to climb) + 山 (mountain).

grammar_points.explanation_en: Indicates that someone wants to do something to realize the state indicated by the adjective.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a lightbulb appearing over Tianming's head with a mountain icon inside.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to express agreement and talk about coincidences.

narration.subtitle_en: The others agree with the plan. [zh:不反对] means 'don't object'. Ke Lin thinks it's a good idea, [zh:不错]. Then he adds that, coincidentally, his German friend Mark also wants to go. The key word here is [zh:正好], which means 'coincidentally' or 'it just so happens that'. It's used when two things fit together perfectly by chance.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 丽莎和雪梅不反对,柯林也觉得这个建议不错,他说正好他的德国朋友马克也想去。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lìshā hé Xuěméi bù fǎnduì, Kē Lín yě juéde zhège jiànyì bùcuò, tā shuō zhènghǎo tā de Déguó péngyou Mǎkè yě xiǎng qù.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Lisha and Xuemei didn't object, and Ke Lin also thought it was a good idea, saying that his German friend Mark also wanted to go.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Here, '不反对' means they agree with the plan.
A common way to say something is good.
Indicates a convenient coincidence.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons of Lisha and Xuemei nodding. Then show Ke Lin with a thought bubble connecting to his friend Mark, with a '正好' stamp.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to express a dilemma by weighing the pros and cons of different options.

narration.subtitle_en: Now they face a problem: how to get there? This sentence demonstrates a classic dilemma using the '[zh:...吧, ...吧]' structure. First option: take a taxi, [zh:坐出租车吧]. The downside is it's too expensive, [zh:太贵]. Second option: take the bus, [zh:坐公交车吧]. The downside is it's too crowded, [zh:人太多, 太挤]. This pattern is perfect for showing you're stuck between two choices.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 可是怎么去呢?坐出租车吧,太贵;坐公交车吧,人太多,太挤。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Kěshì zěnme qù ne? Zuò chūzūchē ba, tài guì; zuò gōngjiāochē ba, rén tài duō, tài jǐ.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: But how should they get there? Taking a taxi would be too expensive; taking the bus would be too crowded.
on_screen_text.notes: Grammar point: Suggesting multiple alternatives with …吧, …吧

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'rental car'.
Short for 公共汽车 (public bus).
Describes a space packed with people.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Used to express a dilemma between two or more alternatives.

segments.visual_notes: Show a split screen. On one side, a taxi with a '$$$' sign. On the other, a bus packed with people.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to present a solution to a problem.

narration.subtitle_en: Ke Lin finds the solution! The phrase [zh:想出了一个主意] means 'came up with an idea'. The verb is [zh:想出], 'to figure out' or 'to come up with'. And what's the idea? [zh:骑自行车] - to ride bicycles! It's presented with an exclamation mark to show excitement.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 柯林想出了一个主意,骑自行车!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Kē Lín xiǎng chū le yí ge zhúyi, qí zìxíngchē!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Ke Lin came up with an idea: ride bikes!

highlight_words.explanation_en: A plan or suggestion.
The verb is 骑 (qí), used for riding things you straddle, like bikes or horses.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a lightbulb over Ke Lin's head, which turns into a bicycle icon.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to list multiple benefits using '又...又...' and '而且', and use the formal structure '有益于'.

narration.subtitle_en: Everyone loves the idea. This sentence lists the reasons why. Biking can [zh:锻炼身体], exercise the body, and also [zh:省钱], save money. The structure '又...又...' connects these two benefits. Then, [zh:而且] adds one more important point: it's beneficial for environmental protection, [zh:有益于环境保护]. [zh:有益于] is a more formal way to say 'is good for' or 'is beneficial to'. Notice [zh:环保] is the short form of [zh:环境保护].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 大家马上就同意了,觉得骑自行车又能锻炼身体,又省钱,而且有益于环境保护。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dàjiā mǎshàng jiù tóngyì le, juéde qí zìxíngchē yòu néng duànliàn shēntǐ, yòu shěng qián, érqiě yǒuyì yú huánjìng bǎohù.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Everyone immediately agreed, feeling that riding bikes is good exercise, saves money, and is also beneficial for environmental protection.
on_screen_text.notes: Grammar point: 有益于 (beneficial to)

highlight_words.explanation_en: A formal way to say 'is good for'. It's a combination of 有益 (beneficial) and the preposition 于 (to).
Often shortened to 环保 (huánbǎo).

grammar_points.explanation_en: A formal structure meaning 'beneficial to...' or 'good for...'.

segments.visual_notes: Show three icons: a flexing arm (锻炼身体), a piggy bank (省钱), and a green planet (环境保护).

segments.teaching_goal: Understand how to describe the start of a journey and a sequence of actions.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence describes the beginning of their trip and what happens after riding for a while. First, [zh:他们早上很早就出发了] means 'They set out very early in the morning'. Then, after riding a long way, [zh:骑了很长的一段路以后], they get tired. The structure [zh:一边...一边...] shows they are doing two actions at the same time: pushing their bikes, [zh:推着车], and chatting, [zh:聊了起来].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他们早上很早就出发了。骑了很长的一段路以后,有点热,也有点累,就下了车,一边推着车,一边聊了起来。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tāmen zǎoshang hěn zǎo jiù chūfā le. Qíle hěn cháng de yí duàn lù yǐhòu, yǒudiǎn rè, yě yǒudiǎn lèi, jiù xiàle chē, yìbiān tuī zhe chē, yìbiān liáo le qǐlái.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: They set out very early. After riding for a long way, they got a bit hot and tired, so they got off their bikes, pushing them while they chatted.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A measure word for sections of things like roads, time, or text.
The action of moving something forward by applying force.

segments.visual_notes: Animate characters riding bikes, then getting off to walk and push them while chat bubbles appear.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn vocabulary related to air quality and make comparisons.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, they notice a difference in the environment. The word for air is [zh:空气]. They say the air here is better than in the city, [zh:比城里好]. They also mention [zh:雾霾], which means 'smog'. The smog is not as serious here, [zh:没有那么严重]. This sentence is a simple comparison highlighting the benefits of being outside the city.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这儿的空气比城里好,雾霾也没有那么严重。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhèr de kōngqì bǐ chéng lǐ hǎo, wùmái yě méiyǒu nàme yánzhòng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The air here is better than in the city, and the smog isn't as bad.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Composed of 空 (empty, sky) and 气 (gas, air).
A compound word from 雾 (fog) and 霾 (haze).
Used to describe the intensity of a problem or situation.

segments.visual_notes: Split screen: one side shows a gray, smoggy city skyline, the other shows a clear, sunny mountain view.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to describe a pleasant view.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a simple, expressive sentence. [zh:到处] means 'everywhere'. So, [zh:到处都是绿色] means 'it's green everywhere'. The second part, [zh:看着真舒服], expresses a feeling. It literally means 'looking at it is really comfortable', which is a very natural way to say that the view is pleasant and relaxing.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 到处都是绿色,看着真舒服!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dàochù dōu shì lǜsè, kàn zhe zhēn shūfu!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: It's green everywhere, which is really pleasant to look at!

highlight_words.explanation_en: Indicates that something is in all places.
Can describe physical comfort or a pleasant feeling from an experience.

segments.visual_notes: Show a panoramic view of green mountains and trees.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn common expressions for exclamation and exaggeration.

narration.subtitle_en: Here we have some very common spoken expressions. [zh:哎呀] is an interjection like 'Oh my!' or 'Oh dear!'. Then, [zh:渴死了] literally means 'thirsty to death', but it's a common exaggeration just like 'I'm starving!' in English. It simply means 'I'm extremely thirsty'. Finally, [zh:都喝完了] means 'finished it all', with [zh:都] emphasizing completion.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哎呀,渴死了,……你们看,一瓶水都喝完了!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Āiyā, kě sǐ le,…… nǐmen kàn, yī píng shuǐ dōu hē wán le!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Oh, I'm dying of thirst... Look, we've finished a whole bottle of water!
on_screen_text.notes: Exaggeration: Adjective + 死了 (sǐ le)

highlight_words.explanation_en: An interjection to express surprise or alarm.
The feeling of needing to drink.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a character looking exhausted with an empty water bottle turned upside down.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn vocabulary related to recycling and rules about littering.

narration.subtitle_en: After finishing the water, the character looks for a [zh:垃圾回收筒], which is a 'recycling bin'. Let's break it down: [zh:垃圾] is 'trash', [zh:回收] is 'to recycle', and [zh:筒] is a 'bin' or 'tube'. The sentence ends with a very important rule: [zh:空瓶子不能随便乱扔]. This means 'empty bottles cannot be thrown around casually', or simply, 'don't litter'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这儿有垃圾回收筒吗?空瓶子不能随便乱扔。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhèr yǒu lājī huíshōu tǒng ma? Kōng píngzi bùnéng suíbiàn luàn rēng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Is there a recycling bin around here? We can't just throw the empty bottle anywhere.

highlight_words.explanation_en: 回收 consists of 回 (to return) and 收 (to collect).
A container, often cylindrical.
The action of discarding something.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a recycling bin. Then show a 'no' symbol over an animation of a bottle being thrown on the ground.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to express agreement and point out an object's location.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's the response. It starts with [zh:对], meaning 'Right' or 'Correct'. The speaker agrees they should be mindful of the environment, [zh:我们应该注意环保]. Remember, [zh:环保] is short for environmental protection. Then, they spot the recycling bin. The phrase [zh:你看] is used to draw someone's attention, like saying 'Look'. The bin is [zh:在树下边], 'under the tree'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对,我们应该注意环保,垃圾回收筒……,你看,在树下边。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duì, wǒmen yīnggāi zhùyì huánbǎo, lājī huíshōu tǒng……, nǐ kàn, zài shù xiàbian.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Right, we should be mindful of the environment. The recycling bin... look, it's under the tree.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Expresses obligation or advisability.
To be mindful of something.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a finger pointing towards a tree with a recycling bin underneath it.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to talk about habits and personal choices for environmental reasons.

narration.subtitle_en: This character shares their personal habit. [zh:我好久没买瓶装水了] means 'I haven't bought bottled water in a long time'. [zh:好久没...了] is a useful pattern for this. [zh:瓶装水] is 'bottled water', from [zh:瓶], bottle, and [zh:装], to pack or contain. This is a great example of how Chinese words are built.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我好久没买瓶装水了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ hǎojiǔ méi mǎi píngzhuāngshuǐ le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I haven't bought bottled water in a long time.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used in expressions like 'long time no see' (好久不见).
A compound word: 瓶 (bottle) + 装 (to pack/contain) + 水 (water).

segments.visual_notes: Show a calendar with many days crossed off next to a plastic water bottle with a 'no' symbol over it.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand how to state a purpose using '为了' and describe a positive suggestion.

narration.subtitle_en: This continues the idea of being eco-friendly. The purpose is stated first: [zh:为了保护绿色的地球], 'In order to protect the green Earth'. [zh:为了] means 'for the purpose of'. The suggestion from classmates is to [zh:自己带水喝], 'bring your own water to drink', and [zh:不买瓶装水], 'not buy bottled water'. This reinforces the theme of the lesson.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我们班有的同学建议,为了保护绿色的地球,自己带水喝,不买瓶装水。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen bān yǒu de tóngxué jiànyì, wèile bǎohù lǜsè de dìqiú, zìjǐ dài shuǐ hē, bù mǎi píngzhuāngshuǐ.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Some classmates suggested that to protect our green planet, we should bring our own water instead of buying bottled water.

highlight_words.translation: in order to, for the sake of
highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to introduce the purpose or goal of an action.
To keep safe from harm or injury.
Literally 'ground ball' or 'earth sphere'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a person filling a reusable water bottle, with a happy Earth icon in the background.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to point something out and ask 'what is it?'.

narration.subtitle_en: The conversation shifts as someone spots something interesting. They agree the previous suggestion was good, [zh:这个建议不错]. Then, using [zh:哎] to get attention, they say [zh:你们看], 'you guys, look'. They point to some [zh:亮亮的东西], 'shiny things', on a roof over there, [zh:那边房子上]. The question is simple: [zh:是什么], 'What is it?'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这个建议不错。哎,你们看,那边房子上有些亮亮的东西,是什么?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhège jiànyì bùcuò. Āi, nǐmen kàn, nàbiān fángzi shàng yǒu xiē liàngliàng de dōngxi, shì shénme?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: That's a good suggestion. Hey, look, what are those shiny things on the roof of that building over there?

highlight_words.explanation_en: Reduplicated as '亮亮' to emphasize the quality of being shiny.
A general term for objects.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a character pointing to a distant roof where something is glinting in the sun. A large question mark appears.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn the word for 'solar panels' and how to make a guess using '吧'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's the answer, or rather, a guess. The shiny things are probably [zh:太阳能板], 'solar panels'. Let's look at this word: [zh:太阳] is 'sun', and [zh:能] is 'energy', so [zh:太阳能] is 'solar energy'. [zh:板] means 'board' or 'panel'. The particle [zh:吧] at the end of the question indicates that the speaker is making a guess. They then add, [zh:我在美国也看见过], 'I've seen them in the U.S. too'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 是太阳能板吧?我在美国也看见过。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shì tàiyángnéngbǎn ba? Wǒ zài Měiguó yě kànjiàn guo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Are those solar panels? I've seen them in the U.S. too.
on_screen_text.notes: The particle '吧' (ba) at the end of a question indicates a guess or supposition.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A compound word: 太阳 (sun) + 能 (energy) + 板 (panel).

segments.visual_notes: Zoom in on the roof to clearly show solar panels. Highlight the characters 太阳, 能, and 板 as they are explained.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the phrase for 'generating electricity with solar power' and express strong approval.

narration.subtitle_en: The conversation about the environment begins with this enthusiastic statement. It says, 'Generating electricity with solar power, that's great!' Let's break it down. [zh:用] means 'to use'. [zh:太阳能] is 'solar energy'. [zh:发电] means 'to generate electricity'. The final phrase, [zh:太好了], is a very common way to express strong approval or excitement, meaning 'that's great!' or 'excellent!'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 用太阳能发电,太好了!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yòng tàiyángnéng fādiàn, tài hǎo le!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Generating electricity with solar power, that's great!

highlight_words.explanation_en: Energy from the sun. '太阳' means sun, and '能' means energy.
Literally 'to emit/generate electricity'. '发' means to send out, and '电' is electricity.

segments.visual_notes: Animate an icon of the sun, then an arrow pointing to a power plant icon, followed by a big thumbs-up for '太好了!'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express a hypothetical 'what if' scenario using '要是...那多...啊!'.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, a character imagines another cool use for solar power. The sentence is: 'If bicycles could also run on solar power, how cool would that be!' The structure [zh:要是] introduces a hypothetical situation, like 'if'. [zh:那多酷啊] is an exclamation expressing 'how cool that would be!'. [zh:酷] is a loanword from the English 'cool', and it's used in the same way.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 要是自行车也能用太阳能发电,那多酷啊!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yàoshi zìxíngchē yě néng yòng tàiyángnéng fādiàn, nà duō kù a!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: If bicycles could also run on solar power, how cool would that be!
on_screen_text.notes: A hypothetical sentence expressing a wish or a cool idea.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to introduce a hypothetical condition, similar to '如果'.
A loanword from English, used to describe something as stylish or impressive.

segments.visual_notes: Show a bicycle with a small solar panel on it, with lines indicating power going to the wheels. Add sparkles and the word '酷!' next to it.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how the character '能' is used to form words for different types of energy.

narration.subtitle_en: You've seen the word [zh:太阳能]. The character [zh:能] here is short for [zh:能量], which means 'energy' or 'power'. It's often attached to a source to name a type of energy. For example, [zh:太阳] means 'sun', so [zh:太阳能] is solar power. Similarly, [zh:风] is 'wind', so [zh:风能] is wind power. You can also have [zh:电能] for electric power and [zh:水能] for hydraulic or water power. It's a very useful pattern to know.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 太阳能 / 风能 / 电能 / 水能
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: tàiyángnéng / fēngnéng / diànnéng / shuǐnéng
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: solar power / wind power / electric power / hydraulic power
on_screen_text.notes: The character 能 (néng) means 'energy' or 'power'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Short for 能量 (néngliàng). It combines with other words to specify the source of energy.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: The character itself depicts components related to 'flesh' and 'ability', hinting at inherent capability or power.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The source for solar power.
The source for wind power.

segments.visual_notes: Display icons for sun, wind, electricity, and water. Animate the character '能' appearing next to each icon to form the respective energy words.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to talk about the global energy crisis.

narration.subtitle_en: The conversation now turns to a more serious topic. This sentence means: 'Many countries in the world are facing an energy crisis now.' Let's look at the key words. [zh:现在] means 'now'. [zh:世界上] means 'in the world'. [zh:很多国家] is 'many countries'. The main point is [zh:能源危机], which means 'energy crisis'. [zh:能源] is 'energy source', and [zh:危机] is 'crisis'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 现在世界上很多国家都有能源危机,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xiànzài shìjiè shang hěn duō guójiā dōu yǒu néngyuán wēijī,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Many countries in the world are facing an energy crisis now,

highlight_words.explanation_en: A compound word: '能' (energy) + '源' (source).
A compound word: '危' (danger) + '机' (opportunity/turning point).

segments.visual_notes: Show a world map with several countries highlighted. An icon representing an energy crisis (like a flashing low-fuel gauge) appears over them.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to talk about saving resources like oil and coal.

narration.subtitle_en: This is the second part of the long sentence. It says: 'If all countries used solar and wind power, think how much oil and coal could be saved!' The structure [zh:如果...那...] means 'if...then...'. The key verb is [zh:节约], which means 'to save' or 'to conserve'. The resources mentioned are [zh:石油], 'petroleum' or 'oil', and [zh:煤], 'coal'. The sentence ends with [zh:啊] to add emphasis and a sense of exclamation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 如果世界各国都用太阳能和风能,那能节约多少石油和煤啊!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Rúguǒ shìjiè gè guó dōu yòng tàiyángnéng hé fēngnéng, nà néng jiéyuē duōshao shíyóu hé méi a!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: If all countries used solar and wind power, think how much oil and coal could be saved!

highlight_words.explanation_en: To use resources economically.
Literally 'rock oil'. '石' means rock, and '油' means oil.
A common fossil fuel.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons for oil barrels and coal piles with a big red 'X' over them, then show icons for sun and wind with a green checkmark.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the word '环保' and its full form.

narration.subtitle_en: This line is a short, enthusiastic completion of the previous thought. The word is [zh:环保]. This is a very common and important word, and it's an abbreviation. It stands for [zh:环境保护], which means 'environmental protection'. [zh:环境] is 'environment' and [zh:保护] is 'to protect'. So when the character says [zh:环保], they are exclaiming that using renewable energy is good for the environment.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 环保!哈哈……
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Huánbǎo! Hāhā...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Environmental protection! Haha...
on_screen_text.notes: 环保 (huánbǎo) is short for 环境保护 (huánjìng bǎohù).

highlight_words.explanation_en: An abbreviation for 环境保护 (huánjìng bǎohù).
highlight_words.character_insight_en: '环' comes from '环境' (environment) and '保' comes from '保护' (protection).

segments.visual_notes: The word '环保' appears in large, green letters. Then, it expands to show its full form '环境保护'.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand vocabulary related to government rules and public spaces.

narration.subtitle_en: The topic shifts to specific government policies. This part says: 'I heard that the Chinese government stipulates [for] offices and public spaces...'. The phrase [zh:我听说] means 'I heard that...'. [zh:中国政府] is 'the Chinese government'. [zh:规定] is a key verb meaning 'to regulate' or 'to stipulate'. Finally, [zh:办公室] means 'offices' and [zh:公共场所] means 'public places'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我听说中国政府规定办公室和公共场所,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ tīngshuō Zhōngguó zhèngfǔ guīdìng bàngōngshì hé gōnggòng chǎngsuǒ,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I heard that the Chinese government stipulates [for] offices and public spaces,

highlight_words.explanation_en: A rule or regulation that is officially set.
'公共' means public, and '场所' refers to a place for a specific activity.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a government building, then an arrow pointing to icons for an office and a park/square.

segments.segment_title: Usage: 场所 vs. 地方
segments.teaching_goal: To differentiate between 'chǎngsuǒ' and 'dìfang' when referring to places.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's look at the word [zh:场所] and compare it with [zh:地方]. Both mean 'place', but they are used differently. [zh:场所] refers to a place for a specific activity and usually needs a modifier, like [zh:办公场所] for 'workplace' or [zh:公共场所] for 'public place'. In contrast, [zh:地方] is a more general term for 'place' and can be used on its own. For example, you can ask [zh:这是什么地方], 'What is this place?'. You wouldn't say '这是什么场所'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 场所 vs. 地方
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: chǎngsuǒ vs. dìfang
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Both mean 'place', but '场所' is for specific activities and needs a modifier.
on_screen_text.notes: Correct: 公共场所 (public place). Incorrect: 公共地方.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to a place for a specific activity, usually requires a modifier (e.g., 办公场所).
A general term for 'place' that can be used on its own.

segments.visual_notes: A split screen. On the left, '场所' with icons for office, gym, etc. On the right, '地方' with a general map icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express 'not higher than' using '不高于' and the term for Celsius.

narration.subtitle_en: Here is the first part of the regulation: 'the heating temperature in winter cannot exceed 20 degrees Celsius'. [zh:冬天] is 'winter', and [zh:暖气] is 'heating'. The key structure is [zh:不能高于], which means 'cannot be higher than'. This uses the preposition [zh:于] for comparison, which is more formal than [zh:比]. Finally, [zh:摄氏] is the word for Celsius. The equivalent for Fahrenheit is [zh:华氏].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 冬天暖气的温度不能高于摄氏20度,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: dōngtiān nuǎnqì de wēndù bù néng gāoyú Shèshì èrshí dù,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: the heating temperature in winter cannot exceed 20 degrees Celsius,
on_screen_text.notes: 高于 (gāoyú) = higher than. 摄氏 (Shèshì) = Celsius.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A formal way to say '比...高' (bǐ...gāo). '于' acts like 'than'.
The temperature scale. It comes from the name of the inventor, Anders Celsius.

segments.visual_notes: Show a thermometer in a winter scene. The mercury is rising but stops at 20°C with a barrier.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express 'not lower than' using '不低于'.

narration.subtitle_en: And here's the rule for summer: 'and the air conditioning temperature in summer cannot be lower than 26 degrees.' [zh:夏天] is 'summer', and [zh:空调] is 'air conditioning'. Similar to the previous line, we see the structure [zh:不能低于], which means 'cannot be lower than'. Again, [zh:于] is used for comparison. This regulation aims to save energy by preventing overheating in winter and overcooling in summer.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 夏天空调的温度不能低于26度。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: xiàtiān kōngtiáo de wēndù bù néng dīyú èrshíliù dù.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: the air conditioning temperature in summer cannot be lower than 26 degrees.
on_screen_text.notes: 低于 (dīyú) = lower than.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A formal way to say '比...低' (bǐ...dī). '于' acts like 'than'.
Short for 空气调节 (kōngqì tiáojié), which means 'air regulation'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a thermometer in a summer scene. The mercury is falling but stops at 26°C with a barrier.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the idiom '举双手赞成' to express wholehearted support.

narration.subtitle_en: This line shows strong support for the energy-saving rule. It says: 'I support this regulation with both hands.' The phrase [zh:举双手赞成] is a vivid idiom. [zh:举] means 'to raise', [zh:双手] means 'both hands', and [zh:赞成] means 'to approve' or 'to agree'. So, literally, it's 'to raise both hands in approval'. It's a great way to say you agree with something completely.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 这个规定我举双手赞成。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhège guīdìng wǒ jǔ shuāng shǒu zànchéng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I support this regulation with both hands.
on_screen_text.notes: 举双手赞成 (jǔ shuāng shǒu zànchéng) is an idiom for 'to wholeheartedly agree'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An idiom that literally means 'to raise both hands to approve'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a character raising both hands in the air with a happy expression.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask 'Do you remember?' and set up a comparison.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker then draws a comparison to their past experience. This part of the sentence asks, 'Do you remember? Our classrooms in the American school...'. The phrase [zh:你们还记得吗] is a straightforward way to ask 'Do you still remember?'. [zh:还] means 'still'. This question serves as a lead-in to describe a contrasting situation, which we'll see in the next part.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们还记得吗,我们美国学校的教室,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen hái jìde ma, wǒmen Měiguó xuéxiào de jiàoshì,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Do you remember? Our classrooms in the American school...

highlight_words.explanation_en: To recall something from memory.
A room for teaching. '教' means to teach, and '室' means room.

segments.visual_notes: A thought bubble appears above the speaker's head, showing a faint image of a classroom.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to describe extreme temperatures and contrast situations.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's the description of the American classrooms: 'In winter, we would sweat wearing just a shirt, and in summer, we'd still feel cold wearing a sweater.' This sentence uses a strong contrast between winter and summer to highlight the waste of energy. Notice the phrase [zh:冷得不得了], which means 'extremely cold'. The pattern 'adjective + [zh:得不得了]' is a common way to express an extreme degree.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 冬天穿衬衫还出汗,夏天穿毛衣还冷得不得了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: dōngtiān chuān chènshān hái chū hàn, xiàtiān chuān máoyī hái lěng de bùdéliǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: In winter, we would sweat wearing just a shirt, and in summer, we'd still feel cold wearing a sweater.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'to emit sweat'.
A structure used to indicate an extreme degree of an adjective.

segments.visual_notes: Split screen. Left side: a person in a shirt sweating in a winter classroom. Right side: a person in a sweater shivering in a summer classroom.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the rhetorical question '可不是吗' and the word '浪费'.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a short but powerful agreement. It says, 'Isn't that the truth! It's such a waste.' The phrase [zh:可不是吗] is a rhetorical question used to express strong agreement. It's like saying 'You're absolutely right!' or 'Tell me about it!'. Then, the speaker adds [zh:太浪费了], meaning 'too wasteful'. [zh:浪费] means 'to waste' or 'wasteful'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 可不是吗,太浪费了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Kě bu shì ma, tài làngfèi le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Isn't that the truth! It's such a waste.
on_screen_text.notes: 可不是吗 (kě bu shì ma) is a phrase used to show strong agreement.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A rhetorical question used to express strong agreement with what someone has just said.
To use something carelessly or excessively.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a character nodding emphatically. The word '浪费' appears next to an icon of an overflowing trash can.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to eating out and personal habits for environmental protection.

narration.subtitle_en: The conversation moves to another eco-friendly habit. This part says: 'I've heard that many people now bring their own utensils when they eat out at restaurants'. [zh:不少人] means 'quite a few people'. [zh:去餐厅吃饭] is 'to go to a restaurant to eat'. The key action is [zh:自己带餐具], 'to bring one's own utensils'. [zh:餐具] is the general word for 'tableware' or 'utensils'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我听说现在不少人去餐厅吃饭自己带餐具,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ tīngshuō xiànzài bùshǎo rén qù cāntīng chīfàn zìjǐ dài cānjù,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I've heard that many people now bring their own utensils when they eat out at restaurants,

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'not few', meaning a significant number.
'餐' relates to meals, and '具' means tool or device.

segments.visual_notes: Show a person walking into a restaurant carrying a small bag with chopsticks and a spoon peeking out.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the important concept of '一次性' (disposable).

narration.subtitle_en: This is the conclusion of the previous sentence: '...to avoid using disposable ones.' The phrase is [zh:不用一次性的]. [zh:不用] means 'don't use'. The key word here is [zh:一次性]. It literally means 'one-time-nature'. It's used to describe things that are designed to be used only once and then thrown away—in other words, 'disposable'. For example, [zh:一次性筷子] are disposable chopsticks.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 不用一次性的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: bú yòng yícìxìng de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: ...to avoid using disposable ones.
on_screen_text.notes: 一次性 (yícìxìng) means 'disposable' or 'single-use'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to things that are discarded after being used only once.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of '一次' (one time) and '性' (-ness, nature), literally meaning 'one-time-nature'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a pair of disposable chopsticks with a red circle and slash over them.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express approval and pose a rhetorical question about consequences.

narration.subtitle_en: Another character agrees with this practice. The line is: 'I think that's a great practice. Otherwise, how many trees would have to be cut down every year?' [zh:做法] means 'way of doing things' or 'practice'. The second part, starting with [zh:要不然], introduces a negative consequence. It means 'otherwise'. The sentence ends with a rhetorical question to emphasize the large number of trees that would be cut, [zh:砍树], to make disposable chopsticks.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我觉得这个做法很好,要不然每年要砍多少树啊?
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhège zuòfǎ hěn hǎo, yàoburán měi nián yào kǎn duōshao shù a?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I think that's a great practice. Otherwise, how many trees would have to be cut down every year?

highlight_words.explanation_en: '做' means to do, and '法' means method.
Used to present an alternative, often with negative consequences.
'砍' means to chop or cut, and '树' means tree.

segments.visual_notes: Show a green checkmark next to reusable chopsticks, then an arrow to a forest of trees with a sad face, and a question mark.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the '除了...也...' structure for adding related information.

narration.subtitle_en: The final line adds another related habit. It says: 'Besides bringing your own chopsticks to restaurants, you also have to bring your own bag for shopping.' This sentence uses the structure [zh:除了...也...], which means 'besides... also...'. It's used to add another point to what was just mentioned. So, in addition to bringing chopsticks, people also bring their own bags, [zh:自己带包], when they go shopping, [zh:买东西].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 除了上餐馆自己带筷子,买东西也得自己带包了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Chúle shàng cānguǎn zìjǐ dài kuàizi, mǎi dōngxi yě děi zìjǐ dài bāo le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Besides bringing your own chopsticks to restaurants, you also have to bring your own bag for shopping.
on_screen_text.notes: The structure '除了...也...' means 'besides... also...'.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure used to add another, similar point.
'带' means to bring, and '包' means bag.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of chopsticks, then a plus sign, then an icon of a reusable shopping bag.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the policy of charging for plastic bags in China and its purpose.

narration.subtitle_en: The speaker agrees with the previous point by saying [zh:对], which means 'Right'. They then explain a common environmental practice in China: supermarkets don't give out free plastic bags. The phrase [zh:不给塑料袋] means 'don't give plastic bags'. You have to buy them, as indicated by [zh:要自己买]. This policy encourages people to reduce waste.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对,现在中国超市不给塑料袋,要自己买,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duì, xiànzài Zhōngguó chāoshì bù gěi sùliàodài, yào zìjǐ mǎi,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Right, supermarkets in China don't give out plastic bags anymore; you have to buy them.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A compound word: 塑料 (sùliào) means 'plastic' and 袋 (dài) means 'bag'.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a supermarket and a plastic bag with a price tag on it.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn about the concept of 'white pollution' and how individual actions can help reduce it.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence describes the positive outcome of the policy. [zh:所以] means 'so' or 'therefore'. Because of the policy, many people bring their own bags, [zh:自己带包]. The speaker wonders how much 'white pollution' this reduces in a year. The term [zh:白色污染] specifically refers to pollution from white plastic products. [zh:减少] means 'to reduce' or 'to decrease'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 所以很多人都自己带包,这样一年不知道能减少多少白色污染!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: suǒyǐ hěn duō rén dōu zìjǐ dài bāo, zhèyàng yī nián bù zhīdào néng jiǎnshǎo duōshǎo báisè wūrǎn!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: So many people bring their own bags. I wonder how much white pollution that reduces in a year!

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb indicating a reduction in quantity or degree.
A term for pollution caused by plastic waste, which is often white. 污染 (wūrǎn) means 'pollution'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a pile of plastic bags shrinking, with a green leaf growing in its place.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the environmental impact of cars, including energy consumption and air pollution.

narration.subtitle_en: The conversation shifts to another source of pollution: cars, or [zh:汽车]. The structure [zh:不但...而且...] means 'not only... but also...'. Cars not only use a lot of [zh:能源], which means 'energy', but they also cause serious air pollution. The phrase [zh:对空气造成严重污染] means 'cause serious pollution to the air'. Here, [zh:空气] is 'air' and [zh:严重污染] is 'serious pollution'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 还有汽车,不但要用很多能源,而且还对空气造成严重污染。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Háiyǒu qìchē, bùdàn yào yòng hěn duō néngyuán, érqiě hái duì kōngqì zàochéng yánzhòng wūrǎn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: And then there are cars. Not only do they consume a lot of energy, but they also cause serious air pollution.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A key noun in discussions about conservation. 能 means 'ability' or 'energy', and 源 means 'source'.
空 means 'empty' or 'sky', and 气 means 'gas'. Together, they mean 'air'.
A common word in environmental topics. It can be a noun or a verb.

segments.visual_notes: Show a car emitting exhaust fumes that turn the sky grey.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn suggestions for an eco-friendly lifestyle and its benefits.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence proposes a solution. It suggests everyone should be like them: [zh:少开车] (drive less), [zh:多骑车] (ride bikes more), and [zh:多走路] (walk more). The structure '少 + Verb, 多 + Verb' is a common way to give advice. The benefits are listed using [zh:又...又...], meaning 'both... and...'. It's [zh:环保节能], which is 'eco-friendly and energy-saving', and also [zh:有益于健康], meaning 'beneficial for health'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 大家都应该像我们这样,少开车、多骑车、多走路,又环保节能、又有益于健康。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Dàjiā dōu yīnggāi xiàng wǒmen zhèyàng, shǎo kāichē, duō qíchē, duō zǒulù, yòu huánbǎo jiénéng, yòu yǒuyì yú jiànkāng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Everyone should be like us: drive less, ride bikes more, walk more. It's both environmentally friendly, energy-saving, and good for your health.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An abbreviation for 环境保护 (huánjìng bǎohù).
An abbreviation for 节约能源 (jiéyuē néngyuán).
A formal way to say 'is good for...'. It's similar to 对...有好处 (duì...yǒu hǎochù).

segments.visual_notes: Use icons for 'no car', 'bicycle', and 'walking'. Then show icons for a green leaf, a power-saving lightbulb, and a heart.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn a common and powerful slogan for environmental protection.

narration.subtitle_en: Here's another strong statement of agreement, starting with [zh:对], 'Right'. The sentence [zh:地球是我们的家] literally means 'The Earth is our home'. This is a very common and heartfelt expression used in China to promote environmental awareness. The conclusion is that we [zh:应该好好保护它], 'should take good care of it'. The word [zh:保护] means 'to protect'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对,地球是我们的家,我们应该好好保护它!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duì, dìqiú shì wǒmen de jiā, wǒmen yīnggāi hǎohǎo bǎohù tā!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Right, the Earth is our home. We should take good care of it!

highlight_words.explanation_en: 地 means 'earth' or 'ground', and 球 means 'ball' or 'sphere'.
A verb that means to defend or guard from harm.

segments.visual_notes: Show an image of the Earth being held gently in two hands.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express 'starting with small things' and understand specific examples of conservation.

narration.subtitle_en: This line emphasizes practical, everyday actions. The phrase [zh:从小地方做起] means 'to start from small places' or 'start with the small things'. The word [zh:比如] introduces examples, similar to 'for example'. The examples given are [zh:随手关灯], which means 'turn off lights when you leave', and [zh:节约用水], which means 'to conserve water'. These are excellent examples of simple, impactful habits.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我们应该从小地方做起,比如随手关灯,节约用水。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi cóng xiǎo dìfang zuòqǐ, bǐrú suíshǒu guān dēng, jiéyuē yòng shuǐ.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: We should start with the small things, like turning off lights when we leave a room and conserving water.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to introduce one or more examples. It's slightly more formal than 比方说 (bǐfangshuō).
A set phrase. 随手 means 'conveniently' or 'without extra effort', and 关灯 means 'to turn off the light'.
A set phrase. 节约 means 'to save' or 'to conserve', and 用水 means 'water usage'.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of a hand turning off a light switch, followed by a hand turning a faucet to a drip.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn a simple and direct way to agree with someone.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a very short and enthusiastic response. [zh:好] means 'Okay!' or 'Good!'. [zh:同意] is a verb that means 'to agree'. Together, it's a strong and positive affirmation, showing full support for the ideas just discussed. It's a common way to end a discussion and confirm a shared viewpoint.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 好!同意!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hǎo! Tóngyì!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Okay! Agreed!

highlight_words.explanation_en: 同 means 'same' and 意 means 'idea' or 'opinion'. So, to have the 'same idea' is to agree.

segments.visual_notes: Show a large checkmark or thumbs-up icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to announce an arrival, propose an activity, and encourage others.

narration.subtitle_en: The scene changes here. [zh:哎] is an interjection to get attention. The structure [zh:都...了] indicates 'already', so [zh:都到了山下了] means 'we've already arrived at the foot of the mountain'. The speaker then suggests, [zh:我们开始爬山吧], 'Let's start climbing the mountain'. They make it a fun competition: [zh:看谁先到山上], 'Let's see who gets to the top first!'. Finally, the line ends with [zh:加油], a universal cheer in Chinese meaning 'Let's go!', 'Come on!', or 'Go for it!'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 哎,你们看,都到了山下了。我们开始爬山吧,看谁先到山上!加油!
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Āi, nǐmen kàn, dōu dào le shānxià le. Wǒmen kāishǐ páshān ba, kàn shéi xiān dào shānshàng! Jiāyóu!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Hey, look, we've reached the foot of the mountain. Let's start climbing. Let's see who gets to the top first! Let's go!

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object phrase: 爬 means 'to climb' and 山 is 'mountain'.
Literally 'to add oil', this is a very common phrase of encouragement or cheering.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Indicates 'already'. It emphasizes that an action or state has occurred, often sooner than expected.

segments.visual_notes: Show a signpost pointing up a mountain trail. Animate the characters starting to hike.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe a group of people all busy with their own separate activities using the 'Verb 1 的 Verb 1, Verb 2 的 Verb 2' structure.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's start with our first grammar point. In the dialogue, we heard the line [zh:上课的上课, 找工作的找工作]. This 'Verb Phrase + 的 + Verb Phrase' structure is a vivid way to say that different people in a group are all busy doing their own things. It paints a picture of a bustling scene where no one is idle. The first part, [zh:上课的上课], means 'those who have class go to class.' The second part, [zh:找工作的找工作], means 'those who are looking for jobs look for jobs.' You can apply this pattern to many situations to describe parallel activities.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他们每天上课的上课, 找工作的找工作。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: tāmen měitiān shàng kè de shàng kè, zhǎo gōngzuò de zhǎo gōngzuò.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Every day, those who had class went to class, and those who were looking for jobs looked for jobs.
on_screen_text.notes: This structure implies that everyone is occupied with their own thing.

grammar_points.pattern: Verb 1 的 Verb 1, Verb 2 的 Verb 2
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Verb 1 de Verb 1, Verb 2 de Verb 2
grammar_points.explanation_en: This structure is used to imply that everyone is occupied doing their own thing; no one is idle.

grammar_points.pattern: 运动场上学生们踢球的踢球, 跑步的跑步。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: yùndòngchǎng shàng xuéshengmen tī qiú de tī qiú, pǎobù de pǎobù.
grammar_points.explanation_en: On the athletic field, some students are playing soccer and some are jogging.

segments.visual_notes: Animate two groups of people splitting off to do different activities (one group to a classroom, another to an office).

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the XYXY form of adjective reduplication to express a desire to achieve a certain state.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, let's look at adjective reduplication. The dialogue says [zh:大家都想轻松轻松]. The word for 'relaxed' is [zh:轻松]. Here, it's repeated in an XYXY pattern: [zh:轻松轻松]. This form is different from the XXYY form like [zh:轻轻松松], which simply means 'very relaxed'. The XYXY form, [zh:轻松轻松], implies wanting to *do something* to achieve that state. So, [zh:想轻松轻松] means 'want to relax a bit' or 'want to do something to unwind'. You can use this for other two-syllable adjectives, like saying [zh:热闹热闹] to mean 'to liven things up'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 大家都想轻松轻松。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: dàjiā dōu xiǎng qīngsōng qīngsōng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Everyone wants to relax a bit.
on_screen_text.notes: The XYXY form indicates a desire to do something to achieve the state of the adjective.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Indicates that someone wants to do something to realize the state indicated by the adjective.
Take a bath/shower to unwind and get comfortable.

segments.visual_notes: Show a character looking stressed, then an arrow pointing to a thought bubble with relaxing activities (reading, music). The word '轻松轻松' appears.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express a dilemma by presenting multiple alternatives using the '...吧, ...吧' structure.

narration.subtitle_en: How do you talk about a dilemma in Chinese? The dialogue gives us a great example: [zh:坐出租车吧, 太贵... 坐公交车吧, 人太多]. This pattern, '[Option 1] [zh:吧], [Reason 1]; [Option 2] [zh:吧], [Reason 2]', is perfect for showing you're stuck between choices. The word [zh:吧] here doesn't mean 'let's'. Instead, it introduces an option for consideration, which is then followed by its pro or con. By listing the downsides of both taking a taxi and taking a bus, the speaker clearly expresses their indecision. It's a very common and natural structure in spoken Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 坐出租车吧, 太贵; 坐公交车吧, 人太多, 太挤。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: zuò chūzūchē ba, tài guì; zuò gōngjiāochē ba, rén tài duō, tài jǐ.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: If we take a taxi, it's too expensive; if we take the bus, there are too many people and it's too crowded.
on_screen_text.notes: This structure is used to weigh the pros and cons of different options, highlighting a dilemma.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Used to express a dilemma between two or more alternatives by stating the pros and/or cons of each.

segments.visual_notes: Show a character at a crossroads with two paths. One path has a taxi with a '$$$' sign. The other has a crowded bus. The character is scratching their head.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the formal expression '有益于' which means 'is beneficial to'.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's discuss a useful formal expression: [zh:有益于]. In the text, it says riding a bike is [zh:有益于环境保护], meaning 'beneficial for environmental protection'. This phrase is composed of [zh:有益], which means 'beneficial', and the preposition [zh:于], which in this context means 'to' or 'for'. So, [zh:有益于] is a more formal equivalent of the structure [zh:对...有好处]. You can use it to talk about the benefits of something in a more written or formal style. For example, [zh:运动有益于健康], 'exercise is beneficial to health'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 骑自行车有益于环境保护。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: qí zìxíngchē yǒuyì yú huánjìng bǎohù.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Riding a bicycle is beneficial for environmental protection.
on_screen_text.notes: 有益于 (yǒuyì yú) is a formal way to say 'is beneficial to' or 'is good for'.

grammar_points.explanation_en: A formal expression meaning 'is beneficial to...' or 'is good for...'. It is equivalent to '对...有好处'.

grammar_points.pattern: 体育运动有益于身体健康。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: tǐyù yùndòng yǒuyì yú shēntǐ jiànkāng.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Sports are good for your health.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a bicycle, then an arrow pointing to an icon of a green planet with a plus sign. The phrase '有益于' connects them.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to use 'Adjective + 于' for formal comparisons, as an alternative to '比'.

narration.subtitle_en: The preposition [zh:于] can also be used for comparisons, especially in formal or written Chinese. The dialogue mentions temperature regulations: winter heating cannot be [zh:高于] 20 degrees, and summer air conditioning cannot be [zh:低于] 26 degrees. Here, [zh:高于] means 'higher than', and [zh:低于] means 'lower than'. This 'Adjective + [zh:于]' structure is equivalent to the '比... Adjective' structure. So, [zh:高于二十度] is the same as [zh:比二十度高]. This is a more concise and formal way to express comparison.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 温度不能高于20度。/ 温度不能低于26度。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: wēndù bùnéng gāoyú èrshí dù. / wēndù bùnéng dīyú èrshíliù dù.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The temperature cannot be higher than 20°C. / The temperature cannot be lower than 26°C.
on_screen_text.notes: The structure 'Adjective + 于' is a formal way to make a comparison, similar to '比'.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Used to compare two things, equivalent to '比...Adjective'. For example, 高于 (gāoyú) means 'higher than' and 低于 (dīyú) means 'lower than'.
Four is greater than three.

segments.visual_notes: Show a thermometer. Animate the mercury rising above 20° with the text '高于20度' and a red 'X'. Then show it dropping below 26° with '低于26度' and another red 'X'.

segments.teaching_goal: To review the key grammar patterns and vocabulary from the lesson on environmental protection.

narration.subtitle_en: Great job! Let's quickly review the key grammar patterns from this lesson. We learned how to describe people busy with different activities, how to express a desire to relax, how to talk about a dilemma, and two formal uses of the preposition [zh:于] for benefits and comparisons. Keep practicing these useful structures!

segments.teaching_goal: Verstehe den Hintergrund der Geschichte: die Charaktere, die Zeit und das Wetter.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Beginnen wir mit dem ersten Satz. Er beschreibt die Ausgangssituation unserer Geschichte. Er besagt, dass Zhang Tianming und seine Freunde seit zwei oder drei Monaten in Peking sind und das Wetter wärmer wird. Die Wendung [zh:已经...了] bedeutet „schon“. Hier heißt [zh:已经两三个月了] „es sind schon zwei oder drei Monate vergangen“. Das Wort [zh:逐渐] bedeutet „allmählich“. Also sagt uns [zh:逐渐暖和了], dass das Wetter allmählich wärmer wird.

content.focus_text: 张天明他们四个人来北京已经两三个月了,天气也逐渐暖和了。
content.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng tāmen sì ge rén lái Běijīng yǐjīng liǎng sān ge yuè le, tiānqì yě zhújiàn nuǎnhuo le.
content.focus_gloss_en: Es sind schon zwei oder drei Monate vergangen, seit Zhang Tianming und seine vier Freunde nach Peking gekommen sind, und das Wetter ist allmählich wärmer geworden.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Ein Adverb, das eine langsame, schrittweise Veränderung im Laufe der Zeit beschreibt.
Wird verwendet, um angenehm warmes Wetter oder eine angenehme Temperatur zu beschreiben.
Ein Adverb, das eine langsame, schrittweise Veränderung im Laufe der Zeit beschreibt.

segments.teaching_goal: Lerne eine Struktur, um zu beschreiben, wie verschiedene Personen mit verschiedenen Aktivitäten beschäftigt sind.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Dieser Satz beschreibt, was alle so gemacht haben. Beachten Sie die besondere Struktur: [zh:上课的上课, 找工作的找工作]. Dieses Muster, „Verb 1 的 Verb 1, Verb 2 的 Verb 2“, wird verwendet, um auszudrücken, dass verschiedene Personen in einer Gruppe alle mit ihren eigenen Dingen beschäftigt sind. Es bedeutet also: „Einige waren mit dem Unterricht beschäftigt, andere suchten Arbeit“. Deshalb haben sie sich seit einem halben Monat nicht mehr gesehen, oder [zh:半个月].

content.focus_text: 他们每天上课的上课,找工作的找工作,半个月没见面了。
content.focus_pinyin: Tāmen měitiān shàng kè de shàng kè, zhǎo gōngzuò de zhǎo gōngzuò, bàn ge yuè méi jiàn miàn le.
content.focus_gloss_en: Jeden Tag waren einige mit dem Unterricht beschäftigt und andere mit der Jobsuche, und sie hatten sich einen halben Monat lang nicht gesehen.

content.notes: Grammatikpunkt: Verb 1 的 Verb 1, Verb 2 的 Verb 2

grammar_points.pattern: Verb 1 的 verb 1, verb 2 的 verb 2
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Verb 1 de verb 1, verb 2 de verb 2
grammar_points.explanation_en: Diese Struktur besagt, dass jeder mit seinen eigenen Dingen beschäftigt ist und niemand untätig ist.

segments.visual_notes: Animation mit geteiltem Bildschirm: Eine Seite zeigt eine Person in einem Klassenzimmer, die andere eine Person bei einem Vorstellungsgespräch.

segments.teaching_goal: Lerne, wie man eine Aktivität vorschlägt und wie die Adjektiv-Reduplikation verwendet wird, um auszudrücken, dass man etwas tut, um einen Zustand zu erreichen.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Jetzt ist Samstag, und alle wollen sich entspannen. Die Wendung [zh:轻松轻松] ist ein gutes Beispiel für die Reduplikation eines Adjektivs. Sie bedeutet „sich ein bisschen entspannen“ oder „etwas tun, um entspannt zu werden“. Tianming macht dann einen Vorschlag. Dabei verwendet er das Verb [zh:建议], das „vorschlagen“ bedeutet. Seine Idee ist es, bergsteigen zu gehen, oder [zh:爬山].

content.focus_text: 今天是星期六,大家都想轻松轻松,天明建议去爬山。
content.focus_pinyin: Jīntiān shì xīngqīliù, dàjiā dōu xiǎng qīngsōng qīngsōng, Tiānmíng jiànyì qù páshān.
content.focus_gloss_en: Heute ist Samstag, und alle wollen sich entspannen. Tianming schlug vor, bergsteigen zu gehen.

content.notes: Grammatikpunkt: Adjektiv-Reduplikation (XYXY)

highlight_words.explanation_en: Als redupliziertes Verb bedeutet es „sich entspannen“.
Kann sowohl als Verb als auch als Nomen verwendet werden.
Eine Verb-Objekt-Verbindung: 爬 (klettern) + 山 (Berg).
Als redupliziertes Verb bedeutet es 'sich entspannen'.
Kann sowohl als Verb als auch als Nomen verwendet werden.
Ein Verb-Objekt-Kompositum: 爬 (klettern) + 山 (Berg).

grammar_points.explanation_en: Zeigt an, dass jemand etwas tun möchte, um den durch das Adjektiv ausgedrückten Zustand zu erreichen.

segments.visual_notes: Animation: Eine Glühbirne erscheint über Tianmings Kopf, mit einem Bergsymbol darin.

segments.teaching_goal: Lerne, wie man Zustimmung ausdrückt und über Zufälle spricht.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Die anderen sind mit dem Plan einverstanden. „[zh:不反对]“ bedeutet „nicht widersprechen“. Ke Lin findet es eine gute Idee, „[zh:不错]“. Dann fügt er hinzu, dass zufällig auch sein deutscher Freund Mark mitkommen möchte. Das Schlüsselwort hier ist „[zh:正好]“. Es bedeutet „zufällig“ oder „es trifft sich gerade so“. Man benutzt es, wenn zwei Dinge zufällig perfekt zusammenpassen.

content.focus_text: 丽莎和雪梅不反对,柯林也觉得这个建议不错,他说正好他的德国朋友马克也想去。
content.focus_pinyin: Lìshā hé Xuěméi bù fǎnduì, Kē Lín yě juéde zhège jiànyì bùcuò, tā shuō zhènghǎo tā de Déguó péngyou Mǎkè yě xiǎng qù.
content.focus_gloss_en: Lisha und Xuemei hatten keine Einwände, und Ke Lin fand es auch eine gute Idee. Er sagte, sein deutscher Freund Mark wolle auch mitkommen.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Hier bedeutet „不反对“, dass sie dem Plan zustimmen.
Eine gängige Art zu sagen, dass etwas gut ist.
Zeigt einen passenden Zufall an.
Hier bedeutet „不反对“, dass sie dem Plan zustimmen.
Eine gängige Art zu sagen, dass etwas gut ist.
Zeigt einen passenden Zufall an.

segments.visual_notes: Zeigt Symbole von Lisha und Xuemei, die nicken. Dann Ke Lin mit einer Sprechblase, die mit seinem Freund Mark verbunden ist, und einem „正好“-Stempel.

segments.teaching_goal: Lerne, wie man ein Dilemma ausdrückt, indem man die Vor- und Nachteile verschiedener Optionen abwägt.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Jetzt stehen sie vor einem Problem: Wie kommen sie dorthin? Dieser Satz zeigt ein klassisches Dilemma. Er nutzt die Struktur „[zh:...吧, ...吧]“. Erste Option: ein Taxi nehmen, „[zh:坐出租车吧]“. Der Nachteil ist, es ist zu teuer, „[zh:太贵]“. Zweite Option: den Bus nehmen, „[zh:坐公交车吧]“. Der Nachteil ist, es sind zu viele Leute, „[zh:人太多, 太挤]“. Dieses Muster ist perfekt, um zu zeigen, dass man zwischen zwei Optionen feststeckt.

content.focus_text: 可是怎么去呢?坐出租车吧,太贵;坐公交车吧,人太多,太挤。
content.focus_pinyin: Kěshì zěnme qù ne? Zuò chūzūchē ba, tài guì; zuò gōngjiāochē ba, rén tài duō, tài jǐ.
content.focus_gloss_en: Aber wie sollen sie dorthin kommen? Ein Taxi wäre zu teuer; der Bus wäre zu überfüllt.

content.notes: Grammatikpunkt: Mehrere Alternativen vorschlagen mit …吧, …吧

highlight_words.explanation_en: Wörtlich „Mietwagen“.
Kurzform von 公共汽车 (öffentlicher Bus).
Beschreibt einen mit Menschen überfüllten Raum.
Wörtlich „Mietwagen“.
Kurzform von 公共汽车 (öffentlicher Bus).
Beschreibt einen mit Menschen überfüllten Raum.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Wird verwendet, um ein Dilemma zwischen zwei oder mehr Alternativen auszudrücken.

segments.visual_notes: Zeigt einen geteilten Bildschirm. Auf der einen Seite ein Taxi mit einem „$$$“-Schild. Auf der anderen Seite ein Bus voller Menschen.

segments.teaching_goal: Lerne, wie man eine Lösung für ein Problem präsentiert.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Ke Lin findet die Lösung! Die Phrase „[zh:想出了一个主意]“ bedeutet „sich eine Idee ausdenken“. Das Verb ist „[zh:想出]“. Es bedeutet „herausfinden“ oder „sich etwas ausdenken“. Und was ist die Idee? „[zh:骑自行车]“ – Fahrrad fahren! Es wird mit einem Ausrufezeichen präsentiert, um die Begeisterung zu zeigen.

content.focus_text: 柯林想出了一个主意,骑自行车!
content.focus_pinyin: Kē Lín xiǎng chū le yí ge zhúyi, qí zìxíngchē!
content.focus_gloss_en: Ke Lin hatte eine Idee: Fahrrad fahren!

highlight_words.explanation_en: Ein Plan oder Vorschlag.
Das Verb ist 骑 (qí) und wird für das Reiten von Dingen verwendet, auf denen man rittlings sitzt, wie Fahrräder oder Pferde.
Ein Plan oder Vorschlag.
Das Verb ist 骑 (qí) und wird für das Reiten von Dingen verwendet, auf denen man rittlings sitzt, wie Fahrräder oder Pferde.

segments.visual_notes: Animieren Sie eine Glühbirne über Ke Lins Kopf, die sich in ein Fahrradsymbol verwandelt.

segments.teaching_goal: Lerne, wie man mehrere Vorteile mit „又...又...“ und „而且“ aufzählt und die formale Struktur „有益于“ verwendet.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Jeder liebt die Idee. Dieser Satz listet die Gründe dafür auf. Fahrradfahren kann [zh:锻炼身体], den Körper trainieren. Außerdem kann man [zh:省钱], also Geld sparen. Die Struktur „又...又...“ verbindet diese beiden Vorteile. Dann fügt [zh:而且] noch einen wichtigen Punkt hinzu. Es ist gut für den Umweltschutz, [zh:有益于环境保护]. [zh:有益于] ist eine formellere Art, „gut sein für“ oder „zuträglich sein“ auszudrücken. Beachten Sie, dass [zh:环保] die Kurzform von [zh:环境保护] ist.

content.focus_text: 大家马上就同意了,觉得骑自行车又能锻炼身体,又省钱,而且有益于环境保护。
content.focus_pinyin: Dàjiā mǎshàng jiù tóngyì le, juéde qí zìxíngchē yòu néng duànliàn shēntǐ, yòu shěng qián, érqiě yǒuyì yú huánjìng bǎohù.
content.focus_gloss_en: Alle waren sofort einverstanden und fanden, dass Fahrradfahren gut für die Bewegung ist, Geld spart und auch dem Umweltschutz zugutekommt.

content.notes: Grammatikpunkt: 有益于 [zh:yǒuyìyú] (zuträglich sein)

highlight_words.explanation_en: Eine formelle Art, „gut sein für“ auszudrücken. Es ist eine Kombination aus 有益 [zh:yǒuyì] (zuträglich) und der Präposition 于 [zh:yú] (zu).
Wird oft zu 环保 [zh:huánbǎo] abgekürzt.
Eine formale Art, „gut für“ auszudrücken. Es ist eine Kombination aus 有益 (nützlich/vorteilhaft) und der Präposition 于 (zu).
Wird oft zu 环保 [zh:huánbǎo] abgekürzt.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Eine formelle Struktur, die „zuträglich sein für...“ oder „gut sein für...“ bedeutet.

segments.visual_notes: Zeigen Sie drei Symbole: einen angewinkelten Arm (锻炼身体), ein Sparschwein (省钱) und einen grünen Planeten (环境保护).

segments.teaching_goal: Verstehen, wie man den Beginn einer Reise und eine Abfolge von Handlungen beschreibt.

narration_track.subtitle_en: Dieser Satz beschreibt den Beginn ihrer Reise und was passiert, nachdem sie eine Weile gefahren sind. Zuerst bedeutet [zh:他们早上很早就出发了] „Sie brachen sehr früh am Morgen auf“. Nachdem sie dann eine lange Strecke gefahren sind, [zh:骑了很长的一段路以后], werden sie müde. Die Struktur [zh:一边...一边...] zeigt, dass sie zwei Dinge gleichzeitig tun: ihre Fahrräder schieben, [zh:推着车], und sich unterhalten, [zh:聊了起来].

content.focus_text: 他们早上很早就出发了。

Die Handlung, etwas durch Kraftanwendung vorwärtszubewegen.
